# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function preview(Request $request)
    {
        $url = $request->input('url');

        $ch = curl_init($url);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION, true);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_TIMEOUT, 10);

        $html = curl_exec($ch);
        $error = curl_error($ch);
        $status = curl_getinfo($ch, CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE);
        curl_close($ch);

        if ($html === false) {
            return response()->json([
                'success' => false,
                'error' => $error ?: 'Unable to fetch preview.',
            ], 400);
        }

        preg_match('/<title[^>]*>(.*?)<\/title>/is', $html, $titleMatch);
        preg_match('/<meta[^>]+name=["\']description["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']/is', $html, $descMatch);
        preg_match('/<meta[^>]+property=["\']og:image["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']/is', $html, $imageMatch);

        return response()->json([
            'success' => true,
            'status' => $status,
            'url' => $url,
            'title' => isset($titleMatch[1]) ? trim(html_entity_decode($titleMatch[1])) : null,
            'description' => isset($descMatch[1]) ? trim(html_entity_decode($descMatch[1])) : null,
            'image' => isset($imageMatch[1]) ? trim($imageMatch[1]) : null,
            'html' => $html,
        ]);
    }
}
